title: 2019 Super League Grand Final

{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox rugby league football match |year = 2019 |title = Super League Grand Final |image = |imagesize = 260 |alt = |home = [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] |away = [[Salford Red Devils]] |home_abbr = STH {{leagueicon|st Helens|13}} |away_abbr = SAL {{leagueicon|Salford city|13}} |home_half1 = 12 |home_half2 = 11 |home_total = 23 |away_half1 = 6 |away_half2 = 0 |away_total = 6 |date = 12 October 2019 |location = Manchester |MoM_title = [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] |MoM = {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Luke Thompson (rugby league)|Luke Thompson]] ({{leagueicon|St helens|13}} [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]) |anthem_title = Headliners |anthem = [[Shed Seven]] |referee = [[Chris Kendall]] |attendance = 64,102 |stadium = [[Old Trafford]] |network = [[Sky Sports]] | commentators = Bill Arthur | commentators2 = [[Terry O'Connor (rugby league) | Terry O'Connor]] | commentators3 = [[Barrie McDermott]] | commentators4 = [[Phil Clarke]] | commentators5 = [[Stuart Cummings]] Jenna Brooks |tournaments = [[Super League Grand Final]] |last = [[2018 Super League Grand Final|2018]] |next = [[2020 Super League Grand Final|2020]] }} The '''2019 Super League Grand Final''' was the '''22nd official Grand Final''' and championship-deciding game of [[Super League XXIV]]. It was held at [[Old Trafford]] in [[Manchester]] on 12 October 2019.{{cite web |url=https://tfgm.com/travel-updates/super-league-grand-final-2019 |title=Super League Grand Final |access-date=18 September 2019 }}{{Dead link|date=January 2022 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} The final was contested by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and [[Salford Red Devils]] (who were appearing in their first ever grand final).{{cite web|url= https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live|title =St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|date=12 October 2019|work=The 42|access-date=14 October 2019}}{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/49998758|title =Super League Grand Final: St Helens beat Salford Red Devils in Old Trafford decider|date=12 October 2019|work=BBC Sport|access-date=14 October 2019}} ==Background== {{further|Super League XXIV}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |- ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Position"| Pos ! style="width:175px;"| Team ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Played"| Pld ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Won"| W ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Drawn"| D ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Lost"| L ! style="width:25px;" abbr="Points for"| PF ! style="width:25px;" abbr="Points against"| PA ! style="width:25px;" abbr="Points difference"| PD ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Points"| Pts |- | 1 ||style="text-align:left"| {{leagueicon|St Helens|12}} [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] | 29 || 26 || 0 || 3 || 916 || 395 || +521 || 52 |- | 3 ||style="text-align:left;"| {{leagueicon|Salford city|12}} [[Salford Red Devils]] | 29 || 17 || 0 || 13 || 783 || 597 || +186 || 34 |} ===Route to the Final=== ====St Helens==== St Helens finished 1st in the regular season, to earn a bye to the semi-finals and a home tie against 2nd place [[Wigan Warriors]]. Saints dominated the first half by scoring four tries to Wigan's one and led 26–6 at half-time with tries from [[Theo Fages]], [[Kevin Naiqama]], [[Luke Thompson (rugby league)|Luke Thompson]], and [[Jonny Lomax]]. Wigan knew they had a job to do in the 2nd half, but this was not to be, as Saints [[Zeb Taia]]'s score and a [[Mark Percival]] double extended the lead to send Saints to their first grand final since [[2014 Super League Grand Final|2014]]. This was also [[Justin Holbrook]]'s final game for Saints at the [[Totally Wicked Stadium]], without losing a single home game all season. He will leave at the end of the season, as he prepares to take on the vacant coaching role at [[Gold Coast Titans]] and will be replaced by [[Kristian Woolf]] for the [[Super League XXV|2020 Season]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49815670|title=Super League: St Helens 40-10 Wigan Warriors - Saints into Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |date=September 27, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/09/10/woolf-confirmed-as-new-st-helens-coach/|title=Woolf confirmed as new St Helens coach|date=September 10, 2019|website=National Rugby League}} {| class="wikitable plainrowheaders" style="text-align:center;margin-left:1em;" !width="25" | Round !width="150" | Opposition !width="60" | Score |- !scope=row style="text-align:center"| Semi-final | {{leagueicon|wigan|16}} [[Wigan Warriors]] (H) |bgcolor=DDFFDD| 32-6 |- | colspan="3" style="background-color:white;"| '''Key:''' (H) = Home venue; (A) = Away venue; (N) = [[Home advantage#Neutral venues|Neutral venue]]. |} ====Salford Red Devils==== Salford finished 3rd in the regular season, to earn an away tie against 2nd place [[Wigan Warriors]] in the qualifying finals, only to lose 18–12. This meant that Salford would get another chance against Castleford in the Elimination-final, this time there was no mistakes, as they went on to win the match 22–0, thus setting up another tie against Wigan in the preliminary final. Salford opened the scoring on 2 minutes, as Lee Mossop had the ball stolen, and Krisnan Inu slotted over a penalty goal from 40m out. Wigan were again penalised in the 7th minute, for offside and Inu slotted over another penalty to make it 4–0. The video referee was called into action in the 13th minute, as Gil Dudson went over for the visitors, but referee Ben Thaler couldn't decide whether it was a double movement or not. The try was awarded and Inu added the extras to give Salford a 10–0 lead after 15 minutes. The visitors extended their lead further in the 45th minute, Joey Lussick crashing over from close range, the try awarded after much deliberation from the video referee. Inu adding another conversion to give Salford an 18–0 lead. Inu added a fifth penalty goal of the evening in the 65th minute, before the Warriors finally got their first score of the game in the 72nd minute, [[Oliver Gildart]] made the initial break, before being captured out by good vision from Sean O’Loughlin, saw the skipper put a kick through for Bevan French, to win the foot race and ease over. Zak Hardaker hit the uprights with the conversion attempt and it was 28–4 to the visitors. Salford won, and earned them a first ever appearance at the grand final.{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49909176|title=Salford Red Devils reach Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors 4-28 Salford Red Devils|work=BBC Sport |date=October 4, 2019}} {| class="wikitable plainrowheaders" style="text-align:center;margin-left:1em;" !width="25" | Round !width="150" | Opposition !width="60" | Score |- !scope=row style="text-align:center"| Qualifying Play-Off | {{leagueicon|wigan|16}} [[Wigan Warriors]] (A) |bgcolor=FFDDDD| 18-12 |- !scope=row style="text-align:center"| Elimination Semi-Final | {{leagueicon|castleford|16}} [[Castleford Tigers]] (H) |bgcolor=DDFFDD| 22-0 |- !scope=row style="text-align:center"| Preliminary Final | {{leagueicon|wigan|16}} [[Wigan Warriors]] (A) |bgcolor=DDFFDD| 28-4 |- | colspan="3" style="background-color:white;"| '''Key:''' (H) = Home venue; (A) = Away venue; (N) = [[Home advantage#Neutral venues|Neutral venue]]. |} ==Match details== {{rugbyleaguebox | bg = #eeeeee | date = 12 October 2019 | team1 = {{leagueicon|st helens|16}} [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] | score = 23–6 | report = [https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/oct/12/st-helens-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-match-report Report] | team2 = {{leagueicon|salford city|16}} [[Salford Red Devils]] | points1 = '''Tries:''' [[Morgan Knowles|Knowles]] 15' [[Zeb Taia|Taia]] 24' [[Mark Percival|Percival]] 50'

1 |{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Jonny Lomax]] | '''{{rlp|SH}}''' !31 |{{flagicon|AUS}} [[Jackson Hastings]] |-style="text-align:center;" !8 |{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Alex Walmsley]] | '''{{rlp|PR}}''' !8 |{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Lee Mossop]] |-style="text-align:center;" !9 |{{flagicon|ENG}} [[James Roby]] | '''{{rlp|HK}}''' !19 |{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Logan Tomkins]] |-style="text-align:center;" !10 | {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Luke Thompson (rugby league)|Luke Thompson]] | '''{{rlp|PR}}''' !10 | {{flagicon|WAL}} [[Gil Dudson]] |-style="text-align:center;" !11 | {{flagicon|COK}} [[Zeb Taia]] | '''{{rlp|SR}}''' !11 | {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Josh Jones (rugby league)|Josh Jones]] |-style="text-align:center;" !17 | {{flagicon|COK}} [[Dominique Peyroux]] | '''{{rlp|SR}}''' !12 | {{flagicon|ENG}} [[George Griffin (rugby league)|George Griffin]] |-style="text-align:center;" !15 | {{flagicon|WAL}} [[Morgan Knowles]] | '''{{rlp|LF}}''' !17 | {{flagicon|IRE|rugby league}} [[Tyrone McCarthy]] |- | style=background:black; colspan=5 | |-style="text-align:center;" !13 | {{flagicon|IRE|rugby league}} [[Louie McCarthy-Scarsbrook]] | rowspan=4|'''Interchange''' !14 | {{flagicon|AUS}} [[Joey Lussick]] |-style="text-align:center;" !16 | {{flagicon|IRE|rugby league}} [[Kyle Amor]] !13 | {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Mark Flanagan (rugby league)|Mark Flanagan]] |-style="text-align:center;" !20 | {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Jack Ashworth]] !15 | {{flagicon|SCO}} [[Adam Walker (rugby league)|Adam Walker]] |-style="text-align:center;" !21 | {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Aaron Smith (rugby league, born 1996)|Aaron Smith]] !16 | {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Greg Burke (rugby league)|Greg Burke]] |- | style=background:black; colspan=5 | |-style="text-align:center;" ! | {{flagicon|AUS}} [[Justin Holbrook]] | '''Coach''' ! | {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Ian Watson (rugby league)|Ian Watson]] |} {{clear}} St Helens opened the scoring through Morgan Knowles, receiving a short ball from Alex Walmsley to crash over to the left of the posts. Coote converted to give Saints a 6–0 lead. The runaway league leaders continued their early dominance, crossing the line again through Zeb Taia following a brilliantly crafted short side scrum play. Coote converted once more to extend the lead to 12–0. The resilient Salford side hit back just after the half hour mark, Jake Bibby finishing off a clever blind side switch in the corner, converted by Krisnan Inu to reduce the score to 12–6 at Half Time. Mark Percival then crossed early in the 2nd half, chasing his own delicately weighted kick to score in front of the raucous Stretford End. Coote converted to restore Saints lead to 12 points at 18–6. Two more Coote penalties extended the scoreline to 22–6 in favour of the Saints, before Tommy Makinson kicked a 40m drop goal to seal a 23–6 win and clinch St Helens their 7th Super League Title. ==References== {{reflist}} {{St Helens - 2019 Super League Grand Final winners}} {{Super League}} {{2019 in rugby league}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Super League Grand Final}} [[Category:Super League Grand Finals|2019]] [[Category:2019 Super League season|Grand Final]] [[Category:October 2019 sports events in the United Kingdom]] [[Category:St Helens R.F.C. matches]] [[Category:Salford RLFC matches]]
